summary: VenuLoQ iteration 89 - Touch swipe bug fix verification. All swipe functionality tests PASSED. The native addEventListener with { passive: false } fix is working correctly.

issues: PostHog analytics throws 'Cannot destructure property clientX' error when touch events are dispatched - third-party issue, not blocking

venue_card_left_swipe.details: Swipe left on venue card images changes transform from translateX(0%) to translateX(-20%), showing next image

venue_card_dot_indicators.details: Dot indicators update after swipe - active dot (w-1.5 h-1.5 bg-white) moves to correct index

venue_card_right_swipe.details: Right swipe returns to previous image, dot indicator moves back

swipe_no_navigation.details: Swiping on images does NOT trigger navigation to venue detail page - URL stays on /venues/search

swipe_boundary_handling.details: Swipe stops at last image (index 4 for 5 images), doesn't go beyond bounds

quick_preview_swipe.details: Quick Preview modal image swipe works - counter updated from '1 / 5' to '2 / 5' after left swipe

login_flow.details: Login with democustomer@venulock.in / password123 successful - redirected to /my-enquiries with 'Welcome back!' message

bug_fix_verification.issue: Photos on venue cards were not swipable on real mobile devices because React's synthetic touch event handlers are passive by default
bug_fix_verification.fix_applied: Switched to native addEventListener with { passive: false } on image container element
bug_fix_verification.verification_result: PASS - Touch events now properly intercepted, preventDefault() works, swipe changes images correctly

critical_code_review_comments: Native touch event listeners correctly use { passive: false } for touchmove to allow preventDefault()
touchRef.current.moved flag with 100ms timeout properly prevents accidental navigation after swipe
Swipe threshold of 25px and horizontal vs vertical detection (dx > 8 && dx > dy) is appropriate
context_for_next_testing_agent: Touch swipe functionality verified working on MobileVenueCard and MobileQuickPreview. Test swipe using TouchEvent dispatch via JavaScript: touchstart → touchmove (>25px diff) → touchend. Check transform translateX changes and dot indicator active class (w-1.5 for active). PostHog analytics error on touch events is third-party issue, not blocking functionality.
